name: The Multicore Era
description: With single-core clock speeds plateauing under thermal and power limits, chipmakers broke the wall horizontally: Nvidia's Tegra 2 put two ARM Cortex-A9 cores on a single die, and the industry followed. Users ran email in the background while streaming music and browsing the web, and a single core simply couldn't context-switch fast enough. The tension was software readiness: mobile OSes and apps had to be redesigned to exploit parallelism, and early quad-core chips often delivered only marginal real-world gains over fast dual-core designs.
